label: Absolute value
note: I think we may want to use `Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Group.Unbundled.Abs` instead.

label: Asymptotic analysis
note: I don't think this is the appropriate Wikidata tag. Asymptotic Analysis is a field of math, we want the entry specifically for "Big O"

label: Lagrange multiplier
note: Do we want to instead tag this with Q598870 "Lagrange Multiplier on a Banach Space" instead? Or maybe we want both tags? There are also several other equivalent formulations of Lagrange multipliers in Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/LagrangeMultipliers, I'm not sure this is the most "canonical" one. See also: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagrange_multipliers_on_Banach_spaces

label: Equivalence class
note: Do we also want to tag `Quotient` or `Quot`? Those are technically in Core, not Mathlib, but are the true type-theoretic foundations of equiv classes.

label: Newton's method
note: This is just the map itself, but says nothing about the iteration behavior / method of finding roots. We want something more like Wikipedia's "Proof of quadratic convergence for Newton's iterative method"

label: Line (geometry)
note: Should we prefer to tag `AffineSubspace` since a line is technically a 1-dim affine subspace (e.g. the image of a `lineMap`)?

label: Coordinate system
note: This does not cover polar/spherical/hyperbolic coordinate systems as discussed in the Wikipedia article. "Coordinate system" is a much more general concept. Instead, we should tag `Basis` with Q189569 (Basis)

label: Duality (mathematics)
note: Duality is a more general concept. We should instead tag `Dual` with Q752487 (Dual Space)

label: Numerical integration
note: Numerical integration is a more general concept. We should instead tag `trapezoidal_integral` with Q833293 (trapezoidal rule)

label: Theta function
note: We should also tag `NumberTheory.ModularForms.JacobiTheta.OneVariable` for the one variable version.

label: Random graph
note: "Random graph" is too broad. We should instead tag with Q605807 (Erdős–Rényi model) since this is the `G(V,p)` model described by `binomialRandom`. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erd%C5%91s%E2%80%93R%C3%A9nyi_model

label: Absolute value
note: Yes, indeed. We want the `abs` that is being used in the `toFun` field here.

label: Lagrange multiplier
note: I'm not enough of an expert in this area to properly review these, but I indeed think this is not a match.

label: Equivalence class
note: I think this is fine. But we could add another `attribute` command below this def, that tags those.

label: Dynamical system
note: Is there also a QID for "flow" specifically? Because then that would be more appropriate.

note: Yes, that probably is the best fit.
